results.name: compute_position_liquidation_check
results.submitted_decl:
use std::collections::HashMap;

pub struct OracleEntry {
    state: HashMap<u64, u64>,
    counter: u64,
    name: Box<str>,
    epoch: u64,
    seq: u64,
    inner: Box<u64>,
    flag: u64,
    settings: HashMap<u64, u64>,
    history: Vec<u64>,
    last: u64,
    floor: u64,
    ceil: u64,
    cap: u64,
}

pub struct Position<'a> {
    users: HashMap<u64, u64>,
    history: Vec<u8>,
    epoch: u64,
    seq: u64,
    state: &'a u64,
    queue: Vec<u64>,
    oracle_entries: &'a [OracleEntry],
}

pub struct OptionUnitQty {
    tag: u64,
    val_lo: u64,
    val_hi: u64,
}

pub struct ClearingHouse<'a> {
    name: &'a str,
    universe_size: u64,
}

(this: &Position<'_>, asset_idx: u64, price: &OptionUnitQty, ch: &ClearingHouse<'_>) -> OptionUnitQty

use std::collections::HashMap;

pub struct OracleEntry {
    state: HashMap<u64, u64>,
    counter: u64,
    name: Box<str>,
    epoch: u64,
    seq: u64,

pub struct Position<'a> {

    epoch: u64,
    seq: u64,

pub struct OptionUnitQty {

pub struct ClearingHouse<'a> {

    universe_size: u64,

results.rationale: option_unit_qty_cmp call confirms 24B OptionUnitQty (3 u64s) for both rcx input and sret output; OracleEntry has &str at +0x38, ptr at +0x58, flag at +0x60; ClearingHouse has &[T;0xc8] at +8.
